stem: A shopkeeper wants a very small program that greets a customer by name.
model_answer:
name = input("Enter your name: ")
print("Hello, " + name)
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Accept OCR pseudocode or Python. Look for input to a variable, a greeting string, and concatenation or equivalent output of the two together.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write a program, using OCR pseudocode or Python, that asks the user for their name and then prints a greeting in the form 'Hello, <name>'.

mark_points.text: reads a value from the user into a variable (e.g. name = input(...))
outputs a greeting that includes the text 'Hello,'
the greeting output includes the value of the variable (concatenation or comma-separated print)

misconceptions.label: Prints a literal name
misconceptions.description: A program that hard-codes the greeting (e.g. print("Hello, Alice")) does not use input; no mark for variable use.
